summary: Frontend regression + new-feature testing for Shannon Engineering. Verified all 4 new pages (/team, /sec-group, /strategic-partners, /safety) load with all expected data-testid markers (4 leaders + 8 discipline heads on Team; 5 sister companies; 20 consultants + 20 clients; 3 SHE pillars + 8 certs). Direct URL / hard-refresh works for every new page. Existing pages (Home, About, Services, Projects, Contact) load with no regressions. Footer carries all 9 nav links and they route correctly. Mobile SEC Group accordion expands/collapses and reveals Sister Companies + Our Team. Top-level Strategic Partners and Safety links work. Zero browser console errors. ONE functional bug found: the desktop 'SEC Group' dropdown trigger combines onMouseEnter (open) with onClick (toggle), so a single click from a non-hovered state opens then immediately closes the menu. Hover-only and click-twice both work; this just breaks the click-to-open flow that the spec explicitly says should work.

ui_bugs.component: Navbar.jsx — desktop SEC Group dropdown trigger
ui_bugs.issue: Single click on the SEC Group button does NOT open the dropdown when the mouse is approaching from outside. The button has both onMouseEnter={() => setSecOpen(true)} and onClick={() => setSecOpen(v => !v)}. Playwright (and a real user moving the cursor onto the button before clicking) fires mouseenter first (state -> true), then the click toggles state -> false. Net effect: the menu opens for ~0ms then closes. A second click is needed to keep it open. Spec requires both hover AND click to work.

action_items: Fix the click/hover conflict on the desktop SEC Group dropdown trigger in /app/frontend/src/components/Navbar.jsx. Recommended fix: remove the onClick toggle and rely on onMouseEnter to open + onMouseLeave on the panel to close (already wired), OR change onClick to setSecOpen(true) (always-open on click) since outside-click already closes it, OR drop onMouseEnter so click is the sole opener. Currently click-to-open is broken because mouseenter pre-sets state to true before onClick toggles it back to false.
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx line 103-113: onMouseEnter + onClick toggling the same state is a known anti-pattern; it produces the click-to-close-on-first-click bug captured above.

Navbar.jsx outside-click handler uses 'mousedown' which is correct, but keyboard users have no way to open the dropdown (no onKeyDown / aria-expanded / aria-haspopup). Accessibility nit, not blocking.
Footer/Navbar both list the same 9 routes — could be DRY'd into a shared nav-config constant to avoid drift when routes change.
Sister-company 'Visit' links are href='#' placeholders (acknowledged as MOCKED).
Contact form submission still MOCKED via setTimeout + sonner toast (acknowledged from iteration_1).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new pages render correctly with all data-testids. Mobile accordion works. The only bug is in Navbar.jsx desktop SEC Group dropdown — onMouseEnter + onClick conflict. To verify the fix: from a fresh state (mouse not on the button), do a single click on [data-testid='nav-link-sec-group'] and assert [data-testid='nav-dropdown-sister-companies'] becomes visible. In Playwright tests it's safer to use page.hover() to open the dropdown until the bug is fixed. data-testids confirmed working: nav-link-sec-group, nav-dropdown-sister-companies, nav-dropdown-our-team, mobile-nav-sec-group, mobile-nav-dropdown-sister-companies, mobile-nav-dropdown-our-team, team-page, sec-group-page, strategic-partners-page, safety-page, leader-card-{0..3}, discipline-head-{0..7}, sister-company-{01..05}, consultant-{0..19}, client-{0..19}, pillar-safety/health/environment, safety-cert-{0..7}, footer-link-{home,about,sec-group,our-team,services,projects,strategic-partners,safety,contact}.
rca of the issue: Root cause: the SEC Group <button> has both an onMouseEnter handler that sets secOpen=true and an onClick handler that toggles secOpen. On any pointer-driven click, the browser fires events in the order: pointerover -> mouseenter -> mousedown -> mouseup -> click. So when a user moves the cursor onto the button and clicks, mouseenter sets state to true, then the click toggles it back to false; the panel renders for an instant and then unmounts. Reproduction: from any page with a non-home navbar (e.g., /about), with mouse not over the SEC Group button, perform a single click on [data-testid='nav-link-sec-group']; observe that the dropdown does NOT remain open. A second click correctly opens it because by then the mouse is already inside (mouseenter does not refire). Mitigation: pick one open/close strategy. Simplest patch: change onClick={() => setSecOpen(v => !v)} to onClick={() => setSecOpen(true)}; outside-click + onMouseLeave already handle closing. Alternative: drop onMouseEnter to make it click-only.
